source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 10. TCF_ADD(HAD1417MCW) 230215 (Rev.5) - Label Update/Attachment 04. Risk Management Report/04-01. Risk Management Report (Doc.No.A-TCF-5-037).docx

document.title: Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting intended use, referenced standards, hazard evaluation, risk controls, residual risk acceptability, and post-production information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the risk management file for ADD and references Risk Management Plan Doc No. A-TCF-5-036 and FMEA table Doc No. A-TCF-5-038 Attachment 1.
keyPoints: ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019 and include risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk evaluation, and production/post-production information review.
The report concludes that overall residual risks are minimized and considered acceptable.
The first post-production information collection covers February 23, 2021 to December 15, 2022 and reports no factors negatively affecting product benefit-risk.

evidence.claim: The document is a risk management report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and the model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector that can generate Images of any part of the body.

evidence.claim: ADD can be used for radiographic imaging of human body parts but must not be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD can be used for radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot, and other internal organs. However, do not use the system for mammography purposes.

evidence.claim: Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The ADD expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: The product contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: The product contains software.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to be portable.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended to be designed portable

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level = Severity x Frequency

evidence.claim: The acceptability table defines Green Zone as acceptable risk level 1 to 4 and Red Zone as unacceptable risk level 5 to 25.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4 Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: Risk evaluation identified 30 hazards, all unacceptable before risk control.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 - 30

evidence.claim: Residual risk evaluation records 30 controlled hazards and 30 acceptable residual risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 30 - -

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are minimized and considered acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Result: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable

evidence.claim: The report concludes that overall residual risk is acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Overall residual risk is acceptable

evidence.claim: The quality management system is based on EN ISO 13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality management system based on EN ISO13485: 2016.

evidence.claim: The first collection of post-production information covers February 23, 2021 to December 15, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Time span: February 23, 2021(Initial CE marking) ~ December 15, 2022

evidence.claim: No factors were identified that negatively affected the product's benefit-risk.
evidence.evidenceText: * No factors were identified that negatively affected the product's benefit-risk.

reviewFlags.reason: Source path references TCF Rev.5 Label Update, while the document text identifies the risk management report as A-TCF-5-037 Rev.2.
Authority signal is general and the document references multiple jurisdictions and regulatory sources rather than one issuing authority.
Some tables were flattened in preprocessing, so row-level interpretation of risk totals has medium confidence.
